c_corp.annual_report_fee: Minimum $50; calculated based on authorized shares or assumed par value capital method
c_corp.minimum_franchise_tax: $175/year (small companies); up to $200,000+ for large authorized share counts
c_corp.notes: Default incorporation state for VC-backed startups. Most investor-friendly laws. Court of Chancery specializes in corporate disputes.

llc.notes: Simple flat annual tax. Popular for holding companies and investment vehicles.

important_notes: Any entity operating in California must pay the $800/year minimum franchise tax, even with zero revenue.
A Delaware corporation operating in California must also register as a foreign corporation (one-time filing) AND pay California's $800/year minimum franchise tax.
LLCs formed in California after January 1, 2021 have the first-year $800 franchise tax waived.
The Statement of Information must be filed within 90 days of formation — $250 late fee and risk of administrative suspension if missed.
BOI report with FinCEN must be filed within 90 days of formation — $500/day penalty if missed.

c_corp.annual_report_due: Within 90 days of incorporation, then annually by the anniversary month
c_corp.minimum_franchise_tax_due: First installment due by 15th day of 4th month after formation; annually thereafter by April 15

llc.annual_report_due: Within 90 days of formation, then every 2 years
llc.minimum_franchise_tax: $800/year (waived first year for LLCs formed after Jan 1, 2021)

foreign_qualification.description: Required for any out-of-state entity (e.g., Delaware corp) doing business in California
foreign_qualification.notes: Delaware corps operating in CA must register as foreign corporations. This is not optional. Failure to register can result in inability to sue in CA courts and back taxes + penalties.

c_corp.annual_report_due: First day of anniversary month each year
c_corp.notes: No state income tax. Strong privacy protections. Less commonly used than Delaware for VC-backed companies.

llc.annual_report_due: First day of anniversary month each year
llc.privacy: No public disclosure of members required
llc.notes: Excellent for privacy-conscious founders. No state income tax. Strong charging order protections. Good alternative to Delaware for non-VC-backed LLCs.

boi_report.penalty: $500/day civil penalty; up to $10,000 fine + prison for willful violation
boi_report.applies_to: All LLCs and corporations formed in the US

83b_election.deadline: Within 30 days of stock grant — NO EXTENSIONS, NO EXCEPTIONS
83b_election.penalty: Ordinary income tax on full vested value as stock vests; potentially hundreds of thousands of dollars in unexpected tax liability
83b_election.applies_to: All founders and employees receiving restricted stock subject to vesting

s_corp_election_federal.penalty: Treated as C-Corp for entire tax year; pass-through status permanently lost for that year

s_corp_election_california.penalty: California treats entity as C-Corp without separate state election; federal Form 2553 alone is not sufficient in California
s_corp_election_california.source: CA Revenue and Taxation Code Section 23800.5

409a_valuation.deadline: Before any stock option grants; refresh at each priced round or annually
409a_valuation.penalty: Option holders face immediate ordinary income tax + 20% excise tax under IRC Section 409A
